name: Fidelity Funding | Hard Money Loans Lender Los Angeles California
description_short: Private hard money lender in Glendale, CA specializing in fast real estate financing for purchases, refinances, fix-and-flip projects, and bridge loans with approval in 48 hours.
description_long:
Fidelity Funding Corp is a private hard money lender based in Glendale, California that focuses on real estate investors and property owners needing fast, flexible financing outside traditional banking channels. The company emphasizes speed and accessibility, with direct access to capital and in-house underwriting capabilities that enable approval within 48 hours and funding within 5–7 days.

The company offers hard money loans ranging from $50,000 to $50,000,000 with loan-to-value programs up to 80–90% on select deals. Their lending products include residential and commercial property purchases, refinances, fix-and-flip projects, bridge loans, foreclosure bailouts, construction loans, and second and third trust deeds. Unlike traditional banks, Fidelity Funding prioritizes property equity over strict credit requirements, making them accessible to borrowers who may not qualify for conventional mortgages.

Fidelity Funding differentiates itself through several key features: zero prepayment penalties, a streamlined 10-minute online application process, transparent loan terms with detailed written offers, and a stated commitment to flexible, investor-friendly financing. Their marketing emphasizes quick closings (under one week in some cases) and personalized service responsive to real estate investment timelines.

As a private hard money lender, Fidelity Funding serves a specific market segment—real estate investors and distressed property owners—rather than primary homebuyers seeking conventional mortgages. Borrowers should understand that hard money loans typically carry higher interest rates and fees than bank mortgages, and the company's testimonials suggest they work best for time-sensitive deals, investment properties, and situations where traditional lending has failed. No independent verification of loan terms, closing times, or borrower outcomes is available on their website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Fast funding timeline: approval within 48 hours and funding within 5–7 days, significantly faster than traditional banks
High loan-to-value programs up to 80–90% on select deals, reducing out-of-pocket equity requirements
No prepayment penalties, allowing borrowers to refinance or pay off loans without extra fees
Equity-based lending model that considers property value over strict credit requirements
Diverse loan products including fix-and-flip, bridge loans, foreclosure bailouts, and construction financing
In-house underwriting and direct access to capital streamline the approval process
Loan amounts from $50,000 to $50,000,000 accommodate both small and large deals
cons: Hard money loans typically carry higher interest rates and fees than conventional mortgages, though specific rates are not disclosed on the website
No transparent rate or fee schedule published; borrowers must apply and receive a 'detailed written offer' to see actual costs
Limited information about underwriting criteria, loan term lengths, or repayment structures on the website
Testimonials are the only evidence of borrower satisfaction; no third-party reviews, ratings, or complaint data provided
Company targets real estate investors and distressed borrowers rather than primary homebuyers, making them inappropriate for standard home purchases
best_for: Real estate investors and fix-and-flip project developers needing fast capital with flexible underwriting
Property owners facing foreclosure who need quick bridge or bailout financing
Commercial real estate buyers and refinancers who cannot qualify for traditional bank mortgages
Borrowers with investment properties seeking short-term bridge loans or construction financing
diagnosis: Fidelity Funding is best for real estate investors and distressed borrowers needing fast, flexible hard money loans for time-sensitive deals, fix-and-flip projects, or foreclosure bailouts. The primary caveat is that hard money loans are significantly more expensive than conventional mortgages due to higher rates and fees, and borrowers should expect to pay a premium for speed and equity-based underwriting; they should only use this type of financing when traditional bank mortgages are unavailable or too slow.
services: Hard money loans for residential property purchases
Hard money loans for commercial property purchases
Bridge loans for real estate investors
Second and third trust deed financing
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1100 Wilshire Blvd APT 2606, Los Angeles, CA 90017
